crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: AUM growth of affordable-housing lenders seen steady at 19-20%
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Growth in assets under management (AUM) of affordable housing finance companies  (A-HFCs) is expected to remain healthy at 19-20% this fiscal and the next, in line with the ~19% growth last fiscal. (Chart 1 in annexure).

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Dairies to churn out 13-15% revenue growth on sustained demand
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Revenue growth of the organised dairy sector is expected to accelerate by 200–400 basis points this fiscal, over a healthy print of ~11% growth estimated for last fiscal. This uptick will be supported by a sustained volume growth of 8–10% and staggered price increases. Volume growth will be driven b

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: West Asia peace deal limits India Inc’s profit impact to ~100 bps
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz following a tenuous US–Iran memorandum of understanding (MoU), if enduring, can materially ease the profitability pressure on India Inc for the rest of this fiscal versus what was envisaged earlier.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Gold-loan lenders ringfenced well from price correction risk
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Strong risk management processes protect the asset quality of the gold loan portfolio of domestic lenders —as measured by ultimate credit loss—from potential correction in gold prices, despite the recent hike in the regulatory loan-to-value (LTV) limit to 85%.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Leasable area of commercial office REITs to rise 25-30% by fiscal 2028
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The leasable area of domestic commercial office real estate investment trusts (REITs)  will increase by 40-45 million square feet (msf) to 190–195 msf by the end of next fiscal, driven by planned asset additions by the existing REITs and the recent listing of a new REIT.

crisil-research.title: Fundamentals fine, but cracks show
crisil-research.description: The global market for senior housing has had a mixed outing this past year. Though fundamentals such as occupancy and absorption have improved amid muted new supply, high profile sales at a loss and broader rationalization of weaker assets have underscored operating and financing risks, especially f

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: IIP momentum sustains
crisil-research.description: Industrial growth, as measured by the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), grew to 5.1% in May from 4.9% in April, above the fiscal 2026 average of 4.3%. Among the headline sectors, electricity and gas was the primary driver of growth on-year (9.9% vs 4.6%). Growth in manufacturing (5.5% vs 6.1%) a

crisil-research.title: Implementing standardized credit risk assessment for bank exposures under Basel III
crisil-research.description: A large European bank was preparing to implement standardized credit risk assessment (SCRA) requirements as part of the EU Banking Package (Capital Requirements Regulation (CRR III) / Capital Requirements Directive (CRD VI) regulatory framework). The bank had significant exposure to a diverse set of

crisil-research.title: Banking Sentimeter
crisil-research.description: India’s banking sector recorded ~13.5% YoY deposit growth in fiscal 2026, however, credit growth remained higher, keeping the CD ratio above 81%. This underscores the need for stronger liability mobilisation. While banks are relying more on term deposits, CASA ratios have declined (~39%) due to cust

crisil-research.title: SectorVector - Pivoting the pipelines
crisil-research.description: Recent months have seen a sharp realignment in India’s liquified petroleum gas (LPG) imports amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

crisil-research.title: US private credit trends, 1Q26 - from resilience to risk awareness
crisil-research.description: The first quarter of 2026 marked a key inflection point for US private credit, as the asset class shifted from being viewed as broadly insulated to facing a more nuanced mix of vulnerabilities and opportunities, with heightened scrutiny around liquidity, valuations, PIK usage, and retail-led redempt

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:25 June 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets rebounded strongly on Thursday morning following strong sales guidance from Micron Technology. South Korea's KOSPI led the tech rebound, while Japan's Nikkei 225 surged 3.5% to trade near 71,649. In contrast, the Hang Seng traded lower by 1.6% at 23,015.

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:24 June 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets showed a mixed and cautious recovery on Wednesday morning. South Korea's KOSPI led a tech rebound, while Japan's Nikkei 225 was flat to slightly lower, recording a decline of 0.38% to settle at 69,524 in early trade. The Hang Seng is trading lower by 0.25% at 23,227.

axis-direct-research.title: Company Update:Kotak Mah. Bank
axis-direct-research.description: We continue to maintain our positive outlook on the bank and maintain our BUY recommendation on the stock with an unchanged TP of Rs 500/share (2.2x FY28 ABV core book), implying an upside of 25% from the CMP.

axis-direct-research.title: Axis Annual Analysis:CreditAcc. Gram.
axis-direct-research.description: We reiterate our BUY recommendation on CreditAccess Grameen Ltd with a Target Price of Rs 1,840/share, implying an upside potential of 30% from the CMP.

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:June 23, 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets are trading mixed on Tuesday morning. The Nikkei opened slightly higher at 72,404 before turning down 0.8% to trade near 71,711, while Shanghai is down 0.2% at 4,153. Conversely, the Hang Seng is trading soft, down 0.6% at 23,620 after a flat open.

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:22 June 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets are trading mixed on Monday morning. Nikkei is trading higher at 72,612, while Hang Seng is trading lower by 1.4% at 23,594, and Shanghai is trading flat by 0.1% at 4,090.

ssga-insights.title: Core portfolio construction principles
ssga-insights.description: What four principles do investors need to consider when it comes to core portfolio construction? Learn about what makes a strong foundation for a flexible portfolio from Matthew Bartolini.

ssga-insights.title: What is the Nasdaq and how does it drive the modern global economy?
ssga-insights.description: What is the Nasdaq? More than an exchange or index, it reflects growth leadership and the companies shaping today’s—and tomorrow’s—economies. See why.

ssga-insights.title: Dissecting equity momentum
ssga-insights.description: Momentum has been the main driver of 2026 equity factor returns, particularly in Technology. But diversification remains essential as market leadership evolves.

ssga-insights.title: Hawkish Fed signals drive market caution
ssga-insights.description: Hawkish signals from the Fed, steady UK policy, and Japan’s rate hike highlight a cautious global outlook amid inflation risks and evolving fiscal uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: Real assets: diversification through conflict
ssga-insights.description: Real assets show diverging correlations during conflict, reinforcing their role as dynamic diversifiers as inflation, rates, and geopolitical shocks reshape portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: ETF Impact Report 2026-2027
ssga-insights.description: What’s next for today’s dynamic ETF market? Get our ETF Impact Report 2026-2027 to uncover top trends and our predictions for the future.

ssga-insights.title: How macro fragmentation may reshape core bond benchmarks
ssga-insights.description: Macro fragmentation is reshaping bond benchmarks. Explore how rising deficits, higher term premiums, and growing Treasury weight reshape core fixed income investing.

ssga-insights.title: What could revive 2026 Fed cut prospects?
ssga-insights.description: Improving labor data and firm inflation keep the Fed on hold, but cooling jobs, softer demand, eased oil risks, or market stress could revive 2026 rate cuts.

ssga-insights.title: Equities overweight edges higher
ssga-insights.description: We modestly increase our equities overweight exposure to 4.0% from 2.5% before, funded by a reduction in US aggregate bonds.

ssga-insights.title: Core-Satellite Fixed Income: Evolving from concept to practice
ssga-insights.description: A Core-Satellite approach to fixed income is increasingly being pursued—see how 3 institutional investors applied this approach to help achieve their objectives

ssga-insights.title: The future of fixed income: From income to engineered outcomes
ssga-insights.description: Read more about the new, modern fixed income. The asset class is increasingly a strategic allocation for building targeted exposures and solutions.

ssga-insights.title: US inflation may be nearing its peak
ssga-insights.description: Inflation remains elevated but may be peaking, while global central banks diverge on policy as growth, sentiment, and inflation outlooks stay uncertain.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: May 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt pushed higher in May, with hard currency leading the way as spreads tightened and local markets navigated a firmer dollar and global uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: India loses EM share as AI trade surges
ssga-insights.description: India’s weight in the MSCI Emerging Markets Index has declined sharply, while Taiwan and Korea gain amid capital shifts toward AI-driven semiconductor markets.

ssga-insights.title: Case Study: An institutional allocator enhances its core-plus fixed income allocation by capturing private credit exposure with an ETF
ssga-insights.description: Case Study: An institutional allocator enhances a core-plus fixed income allocation by capturing private credit exposure with an ETF.

ssga-insights.title: Introducing Trump Accounts: How to jumpstart investing for your children
ssga-insights.description: Learn how Trump Accounts work: a new child-focused investment account designed to support early, tax-advantaged, long-term investing.

ssga-insights.title: Growth takes lead in FX markets
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitics and growth divergence reshape FX markets, as investors refocus on growth, inflation, and policy, driving clearer differentiation across currencies.

ssga-insights.title: Mega-cap IPOs: Implications for institutional investors and index managers
ssga-insights.description: A potential wave of mega-cap IPOs in 2026, including large venture-backed firms, could reshape global and US equity benchmarks.

ssga-insights.title: Investing in AI through sectors
ssga-insights.description: Learn how AI is showing up across different sectors, the investment opportunities, and the implications for investors as they construct portfolios to target AI growth.

ssga-insights.title: A smarter core: An Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios
ssga-insights.description: Is there a more thoughtful way to anchor your portfolio and deliver ballast—while improving the efficiency of returns beyond the traditional satellite approach? Explore an Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: Gold 2026 Midyear Outlook: A tug-of-war between tactical and structural momentum
ssga-insights.description: Structural support for gold remains intact, with prices potentially reaching $5,500/oz by year-end. Record government debt, elevated stock-bond correlations, strong physical demand, and low fund ownership underpin the outlook, though energy-driven inflation and hawkish Fed and G10 policy pose near-t

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: India’s wholesale price inflation (WPI) accelerated to a 42-month high of 8.3% YoY in April from 3.88% YoY in March, driven by a sharp rise in crude oil, natural gas, food and fuel prices alongside a favourable base effect. Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.title: German inflation drops to lowest level since start of Middle East war
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: German inflation slowed again in June and shows very few signs of any knock-on effects from higher energy prices so far

ing-think-rss.title: Italian inflation slows slightly in June on food and services
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: The deceleration in Italy's inflation rate is largely due to lower fresh food prices and services inflation, while energy inflation inched up. If the normalisation in oil and gas prices holds – even if not on a straightforward path – headline inflation could hover in the low 3% area over the

ing-think-rss.title: Czech expansion continues at a milder pace
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: Czech real GDP growth was confirmed at 2.2%, while private consumption and fixed investment dynamics were revised downwards. Solid incomes could still propel consumer spending, but labour market difficulties determine the direction here. ETS2 is planned for 2028, arguably

ing-think-rss.title: USD/JPY: Back to the 1980s
ing-think-rss.description: JAPAN: In breaking above the 2024 highs near 162, USD/JPY has returned to levels not seen since the 1980s. Traders continue to second-guess when and where the Bank of Japan will intervene again, but the outlook for successful intervention remains poor

ing-think-rss.title: Indonesia’s reported quota rethink caps nickel rally
ing-think-rss.description: Nickel prices have retreated as the market reassesses whether Indonesia will follow through with the supply tightening that underpinned this year’s rally. Reports last week that the government may increase this year’s mining quota prompted the market to question whether ore supply will be as constr

ing-think-rss.title: Polish inflation returns to the central bank’s target in June
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: Polish CPI inflation surprised to the downside for the second month in a row. It has now reached the National Bank of Poland's target of 2.5%, with food prices continuing to fall. Markets had already priced out rate hikes ahead of the June flash CPI, but some investors ma

ing-think-rss.title: German labour market weakening takes a pause in June
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: The June drop in unemployment is a welcome positive surprise, but is unlikely to set a new trend

ing-think-rss.title: French inflation surprises on the downside
ing-think-rss.description: FRANCE: Inflation in France came in lower than expected in June. It is expected to pick up again in the coming months, but only to a limited extent

ing-think-rss.title: FX Daily: No doves in Sintra
ing-think-rss.description: We expect communication by ECB speakers in Sintra to broadly endorse expectations for another hike this year after Lagarde’s measured opening remarks. The dollar has continued to hand back some gains, with focus on data today ahead of Warsh's Sintra speech tomorrow, which is expected to be hawkish.

ing-think-rss.title: Rates Spark: Too early for the doves
ing-think-rss.description: Oil is back near pre-war levels, but there are reasons to believe the market is too optimistic over the speed and sustainability of the supply recovery. Rates’ oil sensitivity has become more muted, but uncertainty argues against a dovish turn from the ECB, keeping a hawkish bias for now and floori

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Spain’s Economy Is Growing Three Times Faster Than the Euro Area
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Spain’s strong fiscal position and ‘structural resilience’ have bolstered GDP at a time when its neighbors are struggling to cope with the global energy shock.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: UK Social Mobility: Remains a Tough Climb but One Worth Pursuing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] Increasing social mobility, an important objective in its own right, would also likely have economic benefits, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Investors May Be Overlooking the UK’s Economic Potential
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] The UK economy is beset by weak growth, soft productivity gains, persistent inflation, and pressure on fiscal policy. However the UK retains important strengths and has opportunities in the medium-term to boost economic growth, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for India’s Stock Market Amid the Middle East Conflict
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Indian equities have historically rebounded after periods of crisis, which may offer attractive entry points for long-term investors in the markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for Data Centers in Asia
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Traditional cloud workloads as well as new AI-first demand are driving growth in data centers in Asia, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Private Markets Are Expected to Have a Growing Role in Data Center Financing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] With planned hyperscaler spending on AI and data centers projected to top $5 trillion by 2030, capital from private market infrastructure and real estate funds will become increasingly important for the AI buildout.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: HALO Stocks in Emerging Markets Could Extend Rally as Heavy Industries Surge
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Emerging-market companies marked by heavy assets and low obsolescence, or HALO, are benefiting as the AI boom drives demand for infrastructure.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Oil Prices Could ‘Grind Lower’ Amid the US-Iran Deal
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Energy] While the recent deal between the US and Iran is likely already reflected in the oil market, prices are likely to gradually decline over time, says Jerome Dortmans, co-head of Global Oil and Products Trading in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why the Fed Is Unlikely to Cut Rates This Year
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Goldman Sachs Research has pushed back its forecast for Fed rate cuts to June and December 2027 (from December 2026 and March 2027 previously).

goldman-sachs-reports.title: South Korea's Growing Role in Humanoid Robot Development
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] While humans still outperform humanoid robots on basic real-world tasks, the race is on to improve the machines. South Korea is well positioned to play a key role in the supply chain and humanoid adoptions, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

jpmorgan-research.title: US–Israel military operation against Iran: Are markets on edge?
jpmorgan-research.description: The conflict could pose an upside risk for oil, gold and aluminum prices — though uncertainties around its longer-term impact remain.
